zh.Petersfield, Hampshire, England: 英國哈普爾比特斯費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Eve Dawtrey於大約1285年出生在英國素可巴爾拉維頓時，她的父親Baron John de Hauterive26歲，母親Elizabeth de la Stane17歲。她於大約1308年與Roger de Shelvestrode在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1354年8月16日在英國越可爾享年70歲。

zh-hans.Petersfield, Hampshire, England: 英国哈普爾比特斯費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eve Dawtrey于大约1285年出生在英国素可巴爾拉維頓时，她的父亲Baron John de Hauterive26岁，母亲Elizabeth de la Stane17岁。她于大约1308年与Roger de Shelvestrode在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1354年8月16日在英国越可爾享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Dawtrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Hauterive (Orne) with fused preposition de. The place-name, meaning ‘high bank’, was rendered in clerical Latin as Alta Ripa.

variants.name: Daughtrey
meanings: English: variant of Daughtry . This surname is common in Sussex and Yorkshire, where the medieval Dawtrey family held lands in the 12th century.

variants.name: Dafter
meanings: English: (i) relationship name apparently from Middle English doughter, dohtor, douter (Old English dohtor) ‘daughter’, often pronounced in early Modern English as dafter. For the name to be borne by a man, such that it became a hereditary surname, is surprising, though there are occasional instances of men being named in this way (unless they are clerical errors). Compare Robertus ffelisdoghter, 1379 in Poll Tax (Wentworth, WR Yorks); Johannes Wendoghter, 1379 in Poll Tax (Clotherholme with Bishopton, WR Yorks); and Ric' doghter Wryght, 1379 in Poll Tax (South Elmsall, WR Yorks). (ii) alternatively Daughter might be a simplified pronunciation of Dawtrey (also spelled Daughtrey), a surname well evidenced in WR Yorks and Sussex, where Doughter and Daughter also occur, in which case the change to Dafter will have arisen through misinterpretation. Compare Johna Dautre, 1541 in IGI (Calne, Wilts), Robert Daughtre, 1585 in IGI (Edensor, Derbys), and George Doughtre, 1647 in IGI (Shrewsbury, Shrops), any of which might be examples of either name.

variants.name: Daughtery
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Dawtrey, see Daughtry and also Daughters . The surname is most common in Yorkshire and Sussex, where the medieval Dawtrey family held lands in the 12th century.

variants.name: Daughtry
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Dawtrey, a habitational name with fused French preposition d(e), for someone from Hauterive in Orne, France, named from Old French haute rive ‘high bank’ (rendered into Latin Alta Ripa). The medieval Dawtrey family had lands in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, and Sussex in the 12th century.

variants.name: Daldry
meanings: Norman, English: apparently a variant pronunciation of Daltry, see Dawtrey .

nameMeanings.name: Eve
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name borne in the Bible by the first woman, created from one of Adam's ribs (Genesis 2:22). It derives, via Latin Eva, from Hebrew Havva, which is considered to be a variant of the vocabulary word hayya ‘living’ or ‘animal’. Adam gave names to all the animals (Genesis 2:19–20) and then to his wife, who was ‘the mother of all living’ (Genesis 3:20).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Eve Dawtrey was born about 1285, in Barlavington, Sussex, England, her father, Baron John de Hauterive, was 26 and her mother, Elizabeth de la Stane, was 17. She married Roger de Shelvestrode about 1308, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 16 August 1354, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eve Dawtrey est née environ 1285, à Barlavington, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Baron John de Hauterive, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de la Stane, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Roger de Shelvestrode environ 1308, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 16 août 1354, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eve Dawtrey um 1285 in Barlavington, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baron John de Hauterive 26 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de la Stane 17. Sie hat um 1308 in England Roger de Shelvestrode geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 16. August 1354 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eve Dawtrey è nata il circa 1285 nel Barlavington, Sussex, England, sua padre, Baron John de Hauterive, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de la Stane, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Roger de Shelvestrode circa 1308, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 16 agosto 1354, nel Yorkshire, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eve Dawtreyが約1285年にイギリススセックスバーラヴィントンで生まれた時，父Baron John de Hauteriveは26歳，母Elizabeth de la Staneは17歳であった。約1308年にイギリスでRoger de Shelvestrodeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1354年8月16日にイギリスヨークシュアイアで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eve Dawtrey(이)가 대략 1285년 영국 수세스 발래빙턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baron John de Hauterive(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de la Stane(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1308년 영국에서 Roger de Shelvestrode(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1354년 8월 16일 영국 요르크실르에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eve Dawtrey nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em Barlavington, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Baron John de Hauterive, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de la Stane, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger de Shelvestrode aproximadamente 1308, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 16 de agosto de 1354, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eve Dawtrey родилась около 1285 г. в местности барлавингтон, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Baron John de Hauterive было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de la Stane, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger de Shelvestrode около 1308 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 16 августа 1354 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eve Dawtrey nació aproximadamente 1285, en Barlavington, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Baron John de Hauterive, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de la Stane, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Roger de Shelvestrode aproximadamente 1308, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 16 de agosto de 1354, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años.
